commit.message:
fix: restore monthly accumulation with actual cycle count denominator

Previous commit over-simplified to just current week. Now:
- Accumulates weekly peaks across billing cycle resets (as before)
- Divides by actual number of cycles seen, NOT hardcoded /4
- First-week users: divided by 1 (shows full weekly value)
- 4-week users: divided by 4 (proper monthly average)
- Multi-plan: per-plan split still works

feat: batch 1+2 features — canned notes, preferred channel, stock-linked parts, repeat service

Features implemented:

- Customer preferred channel: whatsapp/email/app/sms on customer record,

commit.message:
Implement autopilot --broadcast: execute lending supply allocations

- Lending allocations: resolve protocol/token → convert USD→wei → supply
- LP/LB/farming/gauge: skip with warning (manual token prep needed)
- Sequential execution, per-allocation error handling
- Remove TODO note from dry-run output
- Remove unused commands: vault, scan, monitor, alert, cdp, whales, positions
- 12 commands remaining, 12 command files

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>

fix: monthly calculation data gaps — 5 root causes

1. Increase MAX_HISTORY 500→2000 with hourly downsampling for entries >24h
   (500 entries at 5min sync = 1.7 days, now covers full 28-day window)
2. History entries always store combined values, not raw per-plan
   (fixes inconsistent scale that corrupted monthly for multi-plan users)
3. Relax reset detection: curW < prevW*0.3 instead of curW < 5
   (catches resets where users immediately start using new week)
4. Use actual weeks elapsed in denominator, not hardcoded /4
   (first-week users no longer show 25% of actual usage)
5. Exclude inactive (>72h) users from team averages
   (stale zero-usage users no longer drag down team scores)

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>

commit.message:
Switch Python backend from pip to UV for dependency management

- Add pyproject.toml with all dependencies from requirements.txt
- Generate uv.lock for reproducible installs
- Update Dockerfile to install uv and use 'uv sync --frozen'
- Update Makefile to use 'uv sync' and 'uv run' commands
- Update shell scripts to use uv instead of pip
- Update .gitignore to exclude .venv/ and build artifacts

UV provides 10-100x faster installs, reproducible builds via lock file,
and single unified tool replacing pip + venv + pip-tools.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Haiku 4.5 <noreply@anthropic.com>

fix: add invalidated to Signal status type to fix build error

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Add feature importance analysis report

Documents two analyses run on the pricing model:
- Part A: SHAP values from existing XGBoost demand model
  (g3_match_significance = 56.9% of demand prediction)
- Part B: GBR trained on optimal_price_increase, R²=0.987
  (target_price_gap 37%, revenue_opp_per_seat 33.5%, sth_resale_margin 28.6%)
- Part C: Season ticket validation by section tier

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Data quality audit — correct inaccurate figures across all sections

Industrial & Land:
- REMOVED incorrect AED 1,558/sqft (was residential listing, not industrial)
- Corrected to: warehouse sale ~AED 295/sqft (Jebel Ali), rent AED 70/sqft (Al Quoz), AED 25-45/sqft (DIC)
- Added 4th card with rent breakdown by location

Hospitality:
- Occupancy: 78% -> 80% (2025 actual, The National/STR)
- ADR: AED 690 -> AED 1,042 (Dec 2025 record, +11.1% YoY)
- RevPAR: +1.3% -> AED 878 (+15.0% YoY, Dec 2025)

Residential:
- Villa avg: AED 2,277 -> AED 1,663/sqft (ready properties, DXB Analytics)
- Apt avg: AED 2,006 -> AED 1,976/sqft (all types, DXB Analytics Jan 2026)

Heatmap corrections:
- Saadiyat: 2400 -> 1900 apt, 2800 -> 2400 villa (transaction-based)
- Al Marjan: 2400 -> 1500 (avg, not branded luxury)
- Al Hamra: 1100 -> 750, Mina Al Arab: 1300 -> 900
- Sharjah communities: reduced 15-30% to match Bayut 2025 report
- Ajman Downtown: YoY corrected to +32% per Bayut

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>

feat: replace hi-lo card visuals with htdebeer/svg-cards French SVG cards

- Install svg-cards npm package (htdebeer/SVG-cards)
- Copy svg-cards.svg sprite to public/ for runtime access
- Add postinstall script to auto-copy sprite after npm install
- Create reusable PlayingCard component at components/shared/PlayingCard.tsx
  - Accepts suit (symbol), value (1-13), faceDown, size (sm/md/lg), glow
  - Maps ♠/♥/♦/♣ → spade/heart/diamond/club
  - Maps value 11/12/13 → jack/queen/king
  - Renders via SVG <use href="/svg-cards.svg#..."> sprite reference

commit.message:
Fix stadium map clipping: compact viewBox + increase iframe height

Root cause: SVG was 1100×960 with height:auto, so at wide screens
(e.g. 1440px) it rendered 1221px tall — overflowing the 870px iframe
and hiding the entire south stand.

Fix:
- H 960→790, CY 450→385 (raise pitch centre so south UB fits)
- South upper bowl bottom now at SVG y=686 (104px clear at bottom)
- North upper concourse top at SVG y=122 (82px clear at top)
- Aspect ratio 1100:790 = 1.39:1 (was 1.15:1)
- At 1440px wide: SVG renders 1034px tall — fits in 1080px iframe
- At 960px wide:  SVG renders 689px tall — plenty of room

commit.message:
feat(costing): cost price methods (WAC/Last Cost/FIFO) + landed cost

Phase 8 of job card enhancements:
- DB: cost_method + cost_history on parts, additional_costs on POs,
  landed_unit_cost on PO lines
- CostingService with 3 methods:
  - Last Cost: unit_cost = latest PO price
  - Weighted Average (WAC): blended cost based on existing + new stock
  - FIFO: tracks cost layers, uses oldest batch cost
- Landed cost distribution: proportional by line value across PO lines
  (freight, customs, clearance, insurance)
- POST /purchase-orders/:id/landed-costs endpoint
- Cost recalculated automatically on goods receipt
- Cost history tracked (last 50 entries) for audit trail
- Per-part cost_method override or tenant default

Add Match Significance breakdown panel below game selector

Decomposes the opaque g3_match_significance model feature (56.9% SHAP weight)
into its constituent flags so analysts can see exactly why a game scores high:

- Baja Cup (+3), Rivalry (+2), Marquee (+2), Season Opener (+2),
  Decision Day (+1.5), Star Player (+1.5)
- Score bar shows 0–12 scale with color gradient (grey → blue → amber)
- Active flags shown as labeled chips with their point contribution
- No-flag state shows "No special flags" in italics

- .statsTile--hireable (teal) applied dynamically; removes old class first

Counter fixes:
- countGet now silently returns 0 on 404/error (new counter not yet created)
- loadCounts splits views + likes into separate try/catch so one failure
  can't zero-out the other
- Views shows — on total failure rather than misleading 0
- onLike does optimistic UI update immediately (number + button state),
  then fires countHit in background — Like button now always responds
  even if counterapi.dev is slow or unreachable

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Fix timer duration bug and add multi-device lock to prevent double counting

Bug fix: saveSession() was using countdown remainder (workMinutes - secondsLeft)
to calculate duration. If localStorage restored a stale state on mobile showing
wrong secondsLeft, this produced wildly inflated durations (e.g. 4 hours from
7 seconds of actual work). Now uses wall-clock time (Date.now() - startTime),
matching what finishTimer() already does.

New feature: active_timer_sessions table acts as a device lock. Only one device
can run a work timer at a time. Includes 30s heartbeat so stale locks from
closed tabs auto-expire after 60s.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>

- Replace all hardcoded /Users/baobao/ paths with $HOME/relative/shutil.which()

- Rename .plist files to .example (machine-specific configs)
- Untrack internal planning docs (.ai/PLAN.md, SLIM_AUDIT, docs/plans/)

License:
- MIT → BSL-1.1 (free for non-competitive use, converts to Apache 2.0 in 2030)
- Update pyproject.toml classifiers and README badge

Documentation:
- Fix README benchmark numbers: align to single verified e2e run (78.94% overall)
- MRR badge 0.91 → 0.86 (actual measured value)
- Remove fabricated Abstain category, correct Multi-hop/Knowledge numbers
- Rewrite CLAUDE.md: remove internal SOPs, competitor names, stale API refs
- Add CHANGELOG.md (v5.0 and v5.1 milestones)

- Add *.sqlite3 to .gitignore

676/676 tests pass.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>

fix(my-bookings): keep modal open on mutation failure, show inline error

Modal footer buttons previously called mutate() then onClose() in the
same synchronous handler, closing the modal unconditionally before the
request completed. On backend rejection the user lost context with no
error feedback.

Fix:
- Move onClose() into the mutate onSuccess callback so the modal only
  closes after a confirmed success
- Render checkIn.error / cancelBooking.error as an inline message above
  the footer buttons so users see the rejection reason without leaving
  the detail view

Add test: "modal stays open and shows error when cancel mutation fails"
